meta.analyst_note: Synthesized by ROUTINE — INSTITUTIONAL RESEARCH v1. Priority on divergence across houses. Numbers, targets and dates preserved verbatim.

SUMMARY: The Iran-US-Israel war (started Feb 28 2026) remains the dominant macro force: IEA calls it the greatest threat to global energy security in history, with 14mb/d shut in and over 1 billion barrels of cumulative supply losses through May. A 60-day ceasefire extension and Hormuz reopening are being discussed but unconfirmed as of May 29. In parallel, an AI-driven earnings and CapEx supercycle has insulated US equities (S&P 500 ~7,550, +8-10% YTD, new ATH) from the energy shock, with Goldman Sachs targeting 8,000 on $340 EPS and UBS targeting 7,900 — even as 30-year Treasury yields breach 5% and the Fed under Kevin Warsh stays on hold amid core PCE running at 3.3% YoY (4.4% annualized, Q1). The deepest house-to-house divergence is on fixed income duration: PIMCO sees great value in bonds and cuts in 2027, while BCA Research downgrades duration to at-benchmark and expects oil inventories to force yields structurally higher through mid-year. A second major divergence is on US strategic position: Brookings/Carnegie argue the US is materially weakened by the war (Iran controls Hormuz, China benefits from US distraction), while GS/UBS equity desks see no structural threat to the AI bull case. On China, Gavekal paints a deteriorating consumer credit picture (household NPLs at 2.15%) while Goldman forecasts Korea KOSPI 9,000 on Chinese green-tech export surge. Crypto is losing the competition for marginal institutional dollars to AI: Bitcoin ETF outflows reached $2.54B over the final two weeks of May, the worst two-week run of 2026.

A_debates.theme: US Equity Outlook: Bull Case vs Duration Risk
A_debates.consensus: AI-driven earnings growth is real and broad (+18% S&P 500 EPS in reported Q1 2026 per BNP Paribas; 'encouraging breadth across sectors'). Technology sector leadership will persist through year-end. Multiple houses remain constructive on US equities despite the energy shock.

divergence.position: BULL. S&P 500 target 8,000 by end-2026; EPS $340 (+24%) in 2026 and $385 (+13%) in 2027 at 21x. Recommends rotating from semis into hyperscalers. Hedge funds at record gross leverage — positioned for upside but raising macro shorts as a hedge.
BULL WITH CAVEATS. S&P 500 target 7,900; Fed cuts Dec 2026 and Mar 2027; recommends rebalancing into short-to-medium duration bonds and diversifying into Japan, China, EM, Switzerland, healthcare.
CAUTIOUS ON DURATION, NEUTRAL EQUITIES. Downgrading duration to at-benchmark on hawkish shift. Markets underpricing oil inventory depletion path. AI bubble risk acknowledged but not terminal.

REBALANCE NEEDED. At record equity highs, urges portfolio rebalancing. Highlights private IG credit ($20T ABF market) as next major asset class. Warns 60/40 is broken; unvested private equity J-curve is the hidden cost of uninvested capital.
TAIL RISK. Hedge funds at record gross leverage 'which has historically been a contrarian indicator.' Raising macro shorts as insurance. Stock markets 'increasingly vulnerable to rising bond yields.'

A_debates.theme: Federal Reserve / Rate Path: On Hold vs Cutting vs Hiking
A_debates.consensus: Fed on hold through at least Q4 2026 under Warsh. Kevin Warsh seen as 'evolutionary not revolutionary' (PIMCO's Richard Clarida). Taylor rule points to policy being 75-100bps too accommodative. Core PCE at 3.3% YoY (4.4% annualized). Software inflation running 9 standard deviations above historical mean (Fed FEDS Notes).

divergence.position: ON HOLD 2026, CUT 2027. Warsh will be rules-based but won't shock the system. Great value in bonds at current yields. Cuts come only when Warsh is confident inflation is under control.

HAWKISH LEAN. Downgrading duration to at-benchmark. Oil-driven inflation plus AI pricing power (software 9 std devs high) argues against cutting; risk the Fed is forced to remain restrictive beyond consensus timeline.

ECB HIKE RISK. For the eurozone, notes 'the risk is that [rates] are hiked in the Eurozone and UK' if inflation remains persistently high. Markets remain tuned to possible US cut but it is a risk scenario, not base case.
CUT DEC 2026 / MAR 2027. Fed has room to cut once energy shock inflation fades. ECB also likely to hike. Base case: 1-2 Fed cuts by end of Q1 2027.
PCE 3.8% YOY. On the high side of consensus for US inflation. Holds OW equities but notes EUR/USD resistance at 1.1730; DXY range 98.6-99.5. Prefers Bunds over USTs and JGBs on relative value.

A_debates.theme: Iran War / Oil: Duration and Price Trajectory
A_debates.consensus: Worst energy supply disruption in history. IEA coordinated largest-ever emergency stock release (Mar 11). Global oil inventories drawn down 250Mb in Mar-Apr (4mb/d pace). Brent approximately 1/3 above pre-conflict levels as of late May (~$93-110 range cited across sources, down from $120-170 peak in March). Ceasefire extension discussions ongoing.

divergence.position: CRISIS STILL ACUTE. 14mb/d shut in; 1bn barrels cumulative supply loss. Saudi/UAE redirecting some exports around the Strait but global inventories depleting 'at a record pace.' TTF natural gas up >40% since late February. 20% of global LNG supply affected.
OIL PRICES MOVING HIGHER. Inventories at multi-year lows by mid-year. US blockade insufficient to force Iran capitulation short-term. Ceasefire fragile; recommends long energy exposure.

US LOSING THE WAR. 'Iran controls the Strait of Hormuz, which means they control access to a major portion of the world's energy supply — it's pretty much a disaster.' (Kagan). Thousands of troops needed to secure the Strait. Trump may choose to walk away.
CEASEFIRE EXTENSION POSSIBLE. US-Iran may extend ceasefire by 60 days; Hormuz reopening under discussion. 'We will not get excited until it actually happens.' Still tracking war as main market mover.

RECOVERY COMPLEX. Gulf export recovery faces multiple logistical hurdles; metals/mining supply chains disrupted with 50% of global seaborne sulphur at risk. Structural cost inflation for mine operators and smelters is 'persistent.'

A_debates.theme: AI CapEx Cycle: Justified Growth vs Credit and Productivity Risk
A_debates.consensus: AI CapEx boom is real and accelerating. Hyperscalers (Amazon, Alphabet, Microsoft, Oracle, Meta, CoreWeave) projected to allocate ~$800bn combined in 2026 (BNP Paribas / Goldman Sachs). Asia chip exports surged 80.7% YoY value in March (Oxford Economics Asia CEI). AI has achieved mainstream adoption in leading sectors.

divergence.position: STRUCTURAL MULTI-YEAR CYCLE. $754bn AI CapEx in 2026 → $905bn in 2027; $7.6T through 2031. Data center power demand doubles to 66GW by 2027. Hyperscalers to outperform semis from here as value shifts to software/inference. AI agents to boost tech sector FCF. Market efficiency declining as AI penetration rises.

divergence.house: PIMCO (Marc Seidner, Jelle Brons, Gene Frieda)
divergence.position: CREDIT RISK GROWING. Hyperscaler capex/operating-cash-flow ratio at 94% — highest on record and approaching levels that have preceded capex digestion pauses in prior tech cycles. $136bn IG bond issuance from AI companies YTD. Private credit market risks 'real.' Sees value in bonds, not in AI credit overweight.

PRODUCTIVITY BOOST STILL MUTED. AI mainstream but 'breadth of adoption matters less than depth.' More than two-thirds of adopting firms use AI for just a few functions. Net boost to GDP growth remains 'close to a few tenths' over the coming year despite capex boom showing no signs of relenting.
AI BUBBLE METER AT 'GOOD'. 0-5% upside over 3-6 months. Not in bubble territory but not early-stage either. Warns against complacency; recommends rotating some profits into non-AI sectors.

MIXED VALUATIONS. Hardware/semiconductor PE ratios elevated but 'well below 1999-2001 bubble peaks.' Software and IT services look 'depressed.' Agentic AI moving from concept to deployment; robotics and embodied AI as next wave. Some hardware earnings (hard disk drives, memory) 'likely unsustainable' as price increases reverse.

PHYSICAL AI UNDERESTIMATED. Humanoid robots to reach $200bn market by 2035; China may have 24M humanoids in workforce by 2035 (4% of labor force). 'Markets may be underestimating physical AI's wealth effects.' New income and wealth creation 'probably underestimated by financial markets currently.'

A_debates.consensus: US-China reached 'constructive strategic stability' at May Trump-Xi Beijing summit — both sides committed to reducing tech escalation spiral and institutionalizing trade/investment boards. China R&D at 2.8% GDP. Yuan at CNY6.80 (strongest since 2023). China battery/EV technology stack leadership cemented at April auto show.

divergence.position: CONSUMER CREDIT DETERIORATION. Household NPL ratio rose to 2.15% (from 1.90% in 2024), double the pre-pandemic rate. China Merchants Bank new bad retail loans doubled YoY to RMB62bn. Urban migrant workers returning to countryside in largest reverse migration since 2008 GFC. Yuan appreciation 'cooling' — financial institutions paring back expectations vs corporate sector still bullish.

BULLISH ON CHINA GREEN TECH EXPORTS. China exports to slow before green energy surge. Korea KOSPI target 9,000 on 300% EPS growth driven by Chinese green supply chain exposure. MSCI Asia ex-Japan profits +62% (UBS).

US FALLING BEHIND. 'America adrift risks falling behind China.' China has definitively arrived as a great power. US military more depleted after 25 years of conflict. China spending nearly 3% GDP on R&D. US loses alliance leverage as Rubio threatens to 'reexamine NATO's value.'

UNEASY STALEMATE NOT BREAKTHROUGH. Trump got trade/investment concessions; Xi got rhetorical concessions on Taiwan and 'constructive strategic stability.' 'China holds enough cards through rare earths and key industrial supply chains to neutralize most forms of US economic pressure.' Both sides continue seeking technological superiority.

RUSSIA-CHINA POWER SHIFT. Putin returned from China without Power of Siberia 2 gas pipeline deal. China increasingly dictating terms to Russia. Iran war did NOT strengthen Russia's negotiating position with Beijing. Russia becoming ever more firmly tied to — and dependent on — China.

A_debates.theme: Bond Yields and Duration: Buy vs Avoid
A_debates.consensus: 30yr UST >5% (highest since 2007). 10yr UST ~4.45-4.57%. UK 30yr gilt approaching 6%. Eurobond yield curve steepened. 'Stocks and bonds are telling different stories' (Deutsche Bank CIO Deepak Puri): AI dominates equities, energy price shock dominates bonds.

divergence.position: BUY BONDS. 'Great value in bonds.' Structurally, after 2026 energy shock fades and Warsh implements orderly disinflation, long-end offers compelling entry. Prefers US IG and securitized credit over sovereign duration.

AVOID DURATION. Downgrading to at-benchmark. Oil-driven inflation plus AI pricing power argues for higher-for-longer. Treasury yields at current levels do not compensate for continued supply/inflation risk.
OW BUNDS OVER USTS AND JGBS. EUR/USD resistance at 1.1730. DXY range 98.6-99.5. European bonds relatively more attractive given ECB credibility vs Fed's inflationary bind.
UK GILT YIELDS CLIMBING. UK 30yr near 6% due to fiscal dynamics + Iran war premium. Foreign investor demand for gilts weakening. This is a structural, not cyclical, move.

US FIXED INCOME OUTPERFORMS EUROPEAN. Performance inversely correlated to duration. Best performers: limited duration, high income, inflation exposure. US outperforms across all maturity and credit buckets. US HY attractive: income return 1% vs 0.8% European HY from Feb 27–Apr 24. Better long-bond performance requires end of Iran conflict.

A_debates.consensus: European PMIs deteriorating sharply (down 5pts on average; all below 50 per BNP Paribas). European equities underperforming US since Feb 27. Eurozone inflation at 3.0% YoY (April, highest since Sept 2023) on energy. NBFI exposure to EGB yield curve steepening flagged by ECB Financial Stability Review.

divergence.position: DISCONNECT PUZZLING. Oil price impact on European vs US economy should not differ dramatically (Brent premium over WTI averaged only 7%). Yet European PMIs in full contraction; US still expansionary. European equities underperforming even on 'apples to apples' comparison. Small caps OW vs large: industrial onshoring, M&A on cheap valuations, infrastructure spending tailwinds.

STRUCTURAL THREAT. Europe risks becoming a 'US or China colony.' China represents 'far greater threat to Europe's prosperity, security, and way of life than the United States.' Europe's ability to resist depends on demonstrating 'unity and resolve.'

HIKE RISK. Euro area bank P/B ratios hit pre-GFC highs then receded on Iran war shock. EGB yield curve steepened. NBFIs with duration exposure most at risk. ECB may need to raise rates if inflation remains persistently high.
NEXT TRADE WAR FLASHPOINT. After Iran ceasefire, next hotspot for US-China trade war could be Europe. Trump may shift tariff pressure toward EU to compensate for China détente.

A_debates.theme: Crypto vs AI: Competition for Institutional Capital
A_debates.consensus: Bitcoin ETF net flows have turned negative for 2026 YTD after two consecutive weeks of outflows totaling $2.54B. BTC down ~5% weekly. Total crypto market cap $2.54T. AI narrative absorbing marginal institutional dollars that would otherwise flow to crypto.

divergence.position: AI COMPETING FOR CRYPTO DOLLARS. 'Investment narratives around artificial intelligence, chip manufacturing, and domestic semiconductor reshoring continue to absorb marginal institutional dollars, leaving Bitcoin to compete for a smaller slice of risk-on flows than at any point since spot ETF launches in Jan 2024.' Wednesday May 28 saw -$723.5M outflows — worst single-day outflow of 2026 and 5th largest spot BTC ETP outflow ever.

RISK-OFF DEEPENING. $1.47B outflows week of May 26, second consecutive negative week. Iran-related risk-off broadened globally — virtually every region recording outflows. Bitcoin YTD flows fell to $2.6B from $3.9B in one week. 'Suggests Iran-related risk-off has deepened despite continued CLARITY Act progress.'

ETHEREUM BRAIN DRAIN. 8 high-profile Ethereum Foundation departures in 5 months (5 in May alone), including co-Executive Director Tomasz Stańczak, Protocol Guild leads Tim Beiko and Dankrad Feist. Prominent Bankless founder sold entire ETH position. Prysm contributor calls it 'textbook case of how to capture an organization.' Structural negative for ETH vs BTC.

GEOPOLITICS RESHAPING COMPUTE. GPU access now geopolitically mediated at defense-treaty level (HUMAIN $600B deal, Stargate UAE 200MW first phase). This creates 'structurally unusual arrangement' that strengthens the decentralized compute narrative beyond historical cost/censorship framing. Japan/Saudi tokenization race while China bans onshore RWA tokenization.

B_calls.call: S&P 500 target 8,000 end-2026; EPS $340 (+24% YoY) in 2026, $385 (+13%) in 2027; 21x multiple. Rotate from semiconductors into hyperscalers as AI value migrates from infrastructure to inference.
Korea KOSPI target 9,000 (+30% upside from late-May levels); EPS forecast for Korean companies +300% driven by green technology export surge to China and AI supply chain leverage.

S&P 500 target 7,900; gold target $5,500; OW Asia ex-Japan equities (MSCI +62% profit forecast); OW EM bonds; rebalance into short-to-medium duration bonds; diversify into Japan, China, EM, Switzerland, healthcare.

Downgrade fixed income duration to at-benchmark from overweight. Oil inventories will hit multi-year lows by mid-year as US blockade fails to force Iran capitulation; go long energy/oil. US blockade insufficient short-term; higher-for-longer rates likely.

Great value in bonds at current yield levels. Avoid excessive AI credit overweight: hyperscaler capex/operating-cash-flow at 94% (highest on record); $136bn AI-company IG bond issuance YTD. Fed on hold through 2026, cutting in 2027. Buy US IG and securitized credit.

OW equities (US, EM bonds); took profit on Asia ex-Japan, rotated to Europe; OW Bunds over USTs and JGBs. EUR/USD resistance 1.1730; DXY range 98.6-99.5. PCE forecast at 3.8% YoY (above consensus). AI bubble meter at 'good' — 0-5% upside over 3-6m.

US data center power demand doubles to 66GW by 2027 from 33GW. AI CapEx: $754bn in 2026, $905bn in 2027, $7.6T cumulative through 2031. Hyperscaler capex as % of GDP highest since dot-com. Overweight utilities with data center power contracts.

European small caps OW vs large caps: industrial reshoring, M&A on cheap valuations, infrastructure spending tailwinds. European PMIs in contraction (down avg 5pts) while US only -2pts. S&P 500 EPS +18% vs MSCI Europe +9% — selectivity key.

Portfolio rebalancing needed at record equity highs. Private IG credit (ABF: $20T addressable market) is the 60/40 successor. Private equity under 'demanding regime' — pricing power matters more than leverage. Warns against holding excess cash due to J-curve of uninvested capital.

El Niño risk rising sharply for H2 2026 and into 2027: NOAA probability of strong/very strong El Niño picking up. If strengthens through summer, EM food price inflation upside risk materializes in H2 2026. EM more exposed (food = larger CPI basket share). Watch rice and grain prices. Complicates EM rate-cut paths.

Private credit under stress: sponsor default cycle emerging, borrower-friendly terms now unwinding. Recommends monitoring private credit NAVs quarterly. Goldman observes 'cracks in private credit' with spreads widening at the margin.

CNY appreciation cooling. Yuan at CNY6.80 — strongest since 2023 but pace of appreciation likely to slow from here. Financial institutions (which dominate FX trading) have pared back appreciation expectations vs corporate sector (still bullish on RMB conversion). Historical model: financial institutions are the better predictor. Do not chase long CNY here.

C_deep_reads.title: Equity Gilt Study 2026: Robots Roll Out, Economies Rewire
C_deep_reads.why_read: 71st edition of Barclays' flagship annual publication. Comprehensive case that humanoid robots represent a distinct and underappreciated wave: $200bn market by 2035, China potentially deploying 24M humanoids (4% of workforce) by 2035. Argues markets are currently underestimating wealth effects of physical AI. Core thesis: previous automation waves (machines, software) left services untouched; humanoids break that constraint.
key_stats: Humanoids alter growth, labor markets, and inflation drivers

C_deep_reads.title: AI Credit Expansion: Assessing the Micro and Macro Risks
C_deep_reads.why_read: The most rigorous bear case on AI credit: hyperscaler capex/operating-cash-flow ratio at 94% — highest on record. $136bn in IG bond issuance from AI companies YTD alone. Documents how AI credit expansion is accelerating at the exact moment yields are elevated, creating a fragility analogous to late-cycle credit expansions. Counterintuitively bullish on bonds as a result.
key_stats: Europe securitization gap as investment opportunity

C_deep_reads.title: The Middle East and Global Energy Markets — Crisis Tracker
C_deep_reads.why_read: IEA's authoritative real-time tracker of the energy crisis. Called it 'the greatest threat to global energy security in history.' 14mb/d shut in; 1bn barrels cumulative supply loss; 250Mb inventory draw in Mar-Apr alone; TTF natural gas +40%; 20% of global LNG supply affected. Charts the unprecedented emergency stock release (Mar 11) and inventory trajectory.

C_deep_reads.title: Has the US Lost the Iran War?
C_deep_reads.why_read: Robert Kagan's Atlantic piece (subscription) and this companion podcast with Michael O'Hanlon and Melanie Sisson is the sharpest strategic dissent from the market consensus that the war is contained. Key claim: Iran now controls Hormuz, which 'completely reversed the power situation in the region.' Thousands of US troops needed to secure the Strait; Trump may choose to walk away. If correct, this scenario is not priced in equities.
key_stats: Iran controls Hormuz = reversed regional power balance
Thousands of US troops needed to secure the Strait
'US is in a worse position than before the start of the war' (Kagan, Sisson, O'Hanlon)

C_deep_reads.title: Tracking Trillions: The Assumptions Shaping the Scale of the AI Build-Out
C_deep_reads.why_read: Goldman's most granular forecast of the AI investment supercycle: $754bn CapEx in 2026, $905bn in 2027, cumulative $7.6T through 2031. Data center power demand doubles to 66GW by 2027. Documents the specific hyperscaler-by-hyperscaler commitments, the power infrastructure bottleneck, and the critical path assumptions. Essential context for evaluating whether the PIMCO bear case on capex/OCF ratios is justified.
key_stats: US data center power demand 66GW by 2027 (vs 33GW today)

C_deep_reads.title: Measurement of Computer Software and Accessories Services: Unprecedented Price Inflation
C_deep_reads.why_read: The data paper behind the most alarming inflation datapoint in the dataset: software pricing is running 9 standard deviations above its historical mean — an unprecedented reading that explains why core PCE is stickier than traditional models predict. Central to the debate on whether inflation is transitory or structural. Fed staff view is this is AI monetization showing up in CPI/PCE data.
key_stats: Software inflation 9 standard deviations above historical mean

C_deep_reads.title: Gavekal Dragonomics: Consumer Defaults Are Climbing
C_deep_reads.why_read: The most detailed dissection of China's consumer credit deterioration in the dataset. Household NPL ratio at 2.15% (vs 1.90% in 2024, vs pre-pandemic ~1%). China Merchants Bank — the sector's best-in-class lender — reporting new bad retail loans doubled to RMB62bn in 2025, equivalent to 1.7% of retail loans. Outstanding household loans DECLINED outright in March and April. Consumer subsidies failing to generate credit demand as creditworthiness is impaired.
key_stats: CMB new bad loans: RMB62bn (+100% YoY)

C_deep_reads.title: Crypto and Blockchain Venture Capital — Q1 2026
C_deep_reads.why_read: Most comprehensive data picture of crypto VC funding. $4B deployed in Q1 2026 across 355 deals — down 50% QoQ from Q4 2025's spike but above 2023-2024 trough. US dominance at 70% of capital (43.5% of deals). New fund formation at lowest since Q3 2020. Institutional dollars flowing into Trading/Exchange/Investing/Lending ($2.6B) and AI-adjacent infrastructure. Documents the bifurcation: crypto startups vs AI startups competing for same dollar pool.
key_stats: New fund formation = lowest since Q3 2020
authors: Alex Thorn (Head of Firmwide Research, Galaxy Digital)

C_deep_reads.title: What if Migration Were to End? Zero Migration Scenario
C_deep_reads.why_read: Quantifies the extreme downside scenario of migration policy reversals: Eurozone old-age dependency ratio rises nearly 9ppts to ~60% by 2060. Output per worker declines in destination economies as productivity gains from migration (knowledge transfer, labor market allocation) are lost. Timely context for immigration policy debates in US and Europe.
key_stats: Eurozone dependency ratio +9ppts to ~60% by 2060 in zero-migration scenario
Destination economies: population, labor supply, and output per worker all decline
Public finances deteriorate in advanced economies
authors: Oxford Economics Scenarios and Macro Modelling Team

D_charts.title: Brent/WTI Premium and European vs US PMI Divergence
D_charts.article_title: View from the Markets: Disparate impact: Why are European equities underperforming?
D_charts.what_it_shows: Exhibit 1: Brent premium over WTI averaged only 7% since conflict start (peaked 20%), refuting the theory that European energy impact is dramatically worse. Exhibit 2: European services and manufacturing PMIs have fallen avg 5pts into contraction, while US PMIs deteriorated <2pts and remain expansionary. The divergence is larger than the oil differential alone can explain.

D_charts.title: AI Credit Expansion: Hyperscaler Capex vs Operating Cash Flow
D_charts.article_title: AI Credit Expansion: Assessing the Micro and Macro Risks
D_charts.what_it_shows: Charts the hyperscaler capex/operating-cash-flow ratio at 94% — highest on record — alongside $136bn in IG bond issuance by AI companies YTD 2026. Visualizes why PIMCO is cautious on AI credit even as they are bullish on bonds generally.

D_charts.title: AI Infrastructure Investment Boom: Sector and Vintage Breakdown
D_charts.article_title: Investment Discipline Amid the AI Infrastructure Boom
D_charts.what_it_shows: Breakdown of AI infrastructure investment across data centers, power/cooling, networking — with vintage year comparison showing acceleration. Context: why PIMCO sees value in securitized AI infrastructure debt rather than equity or unsecured credit.

D_charts.title: Supply Shocks + AI Demand: Dual Forces Blurring Fed's Inflation Signals
D_charts.article_title: Macro Signposts: Supply Shocks and AI-Related Demand Blur Inflation Signals for the Fed
D_charts.what_it_shows: Decomposition of current inflation into supply-shock component (energy/Iran war) vs AI-demand component (software pricing), visualizing why the Fed cannot cut rates: both are inflationary but require different policy responses. Core PCE 3.3% YoY with software at 9 std devs above mean.

D_charts.title: After 60/40: The Hidden Cost of Uninvested Capital Through the J-Curve
D_charts.article_title: After 60/40: The Hidden Cost of Uninvested Capital Through the J-Curve
D_charts.what_it_shows: Illustrates how the traditional 60/40 portfolio fails in the current stagflationary environment, and quantifies the opportunity cost of remaining in liquid public markets vs deploying into private IG credit. Charts the J-curve timing and net-return differential.

D_charts.title: US R&D Investment Leadership vs China, Japan, Germany
D_charts.article_title: Take Two: UN reduces its global growth forecast
D_charts.what_it_shows: Visualizes R&D spending by major tech companies ($800bn projected allocation from Amazon, Alphabet, Microsoft, Oracle, Meta, CoreWeave, etc.) vs global economy leaders. US dominant share vs China, Japan, Germany. Context: why AI CapEx maintains its structural momentum even during geopolitical shocks.

D_charts.title: HSBC Gamechangers: AI's Second-Order Economic Impact — Three Scenarios
D_charts.article_title: Gamechangers: The next stage of AI's impact on the economy
D_charts.what_it_shows: Visualization of Fed Governor Barr's three AI adoption scenarios (gradual/rapid/stall) applied to economic outcomes. Charts current adoption vs productivity impact gap — the 'missing second-round macro effects' puzzle. Key for assessing how long before AI CapEx translates to measured productivity.

D_charts.title: Vanguard: Geopolitical Shock Market Volatility and Investor Behavior
D_charts.what_it_shows: Historical analysis of investor behavior during geopolitical shocks (WWII, 9/11, Gulf Wars, COVID). Charts VIX response and net equity outflows from passive funds. Key message: disciplined investors who held through prior shocks outperformed those who sold. Relevant counter-signal to Bitcoin and equity caution narratives.

D_charts.title: Oxford Economics Asia Chip Export Index: 80.7% YoY Value Growth (March 2026)
D_charts.article_title: Asia Chip Export Index May 2026: From consumer cycles to compute demand
D_charts.what_it_shows: The Oxford Economics CEI shows Asia's chip exports surged 80.7% YoY in value and 28.3% in volume in March 2026 — the widening gap between value and volume reflecting structural pricing power in advanced AI chips. Shifts the tech cycle interpretation: less dependent on consumer device replacement (traditional cycle) and more dependent on cloud capex/enterprise AI adoption (new cycle). Adds 10-15ppts to Asian export growth forecast.

house_views.goldman_sachs: S&P 500 target 8,000 (EPS $340 +24%); hyperscalers OW semis; Korea KOSPI 9,000; AI CapEx $754bn this year, $7.6T through 2031; UK gilt 30yr approaching 6%; private credit under stress; hedge funds at record leverage.
house_views.jpmorgan: Thin teasers in dataset — flagged topics: Kevin Warsh transition, US-China outlook, midyear equity outlook, inflation trajectory. No substantive calls extractable.
house_views.bofa: Consumer spending +4.8% YoY (April); K-shaped consumer (upper-income +6% wages vs lower-income strained); gas spending +31%; small business profitability -1.3% YoY; credit card utilization elevated.
house_views.ubs_cio: S&P 500 target 7,900; Fed cuts Dec 2026/Mar 2027; ECB to hike; gold target $5,500; MSCI Asia ex-Japan profits +62%; OW short-medium duration bonds; diversify into Japan/China/EM/Switzerland/healthcare.
house_views.barclays: Humanoid robots $200bn market by 2035; China 24M humanoids = 4% of workforce by 2035; AI going economy-wide (Episode 33); bond sell-off driven by energy not AI; capital markets selective not closed.
house_views.deutsche_bank: Stocks and bonds telling different stories: AI dominates equity narrative, energy price shock dominates bond market. S&P 500 mid-2027 target upgraded at May CIO Day. Germany/Eurozone growth near potential on fiscal stimulus.
house_views.citi_gps: India GDP $4T → potential $30T by 2047; 8-9% annual growth needed; mortgage lock-in effect limiting US housing mobility; family office AI adoption at 22% (from 13% in 2024).
house_views.hsbc: El Niño risk rising for H2 2026/2027; EM food inflation upside. Private market investors 54% bullish (vs 43% of public investors). AI impact on labor still in scenario-planning phase (3 scenarios: gradual/rapid/stall). Central bank reserve managers reassessing currency allocations.
house_views.bnp_paribas: European PMIs in contraction (down avg 5pts); US only -2pts; European equities underperforming despite oil premium only 7% over WTI; S&P 500 EPS +18% vs MSCI Europe +9%. OW European small caps; OW US HY for income. ECB hike risk if inflation persists. China tech self-sufficiency = top-down policy-driven investment approach.
house_views.bca_research: Downgrading duration to at-benchmark; oil prices to move higher (inventories at multi-year lows by mid-year); Eurobonds structural necessity; AI transformative but existential risks. Hawkish rate view.
house_views.pimco: Warsh evolutionary not revolutionary; Fed on hold through 2026, cuts in 2027; AI credit risks real (94% capex/OCF, $136bn IG issuance YTD); great value in bonds; Europe securitization gap as opportunity; private credit fragility.
house_views.apollo: PE in demanding regime; 60/40 broken; private IG credit ($20T ABF) is successor; J-curve cost of uninvested capital is underappreciated; portfolio rebalancing needed at record equity highs.
house_views.bridgewater: Stagflationary environment deepens; gold as structural diversifier; AI-driven productivity gains insufficient to offset energy/geopolitical supply shock in near term.
house_views.blackrock: AI infrastructure investment as multi-year structural theme; geopolitical fragmentation increasing; portfolio repositioning toward real assets and private credit warranted.
house_views.standard_chartered: OW equities (US, EM bonds); profit-taking on Asia ex-Japan → Europe; AI bubble meter 'good' (0-5% upside 3-6m); PCE 3.8%; DXY 98.6-99.5; EUR/USD resistance 1.1730; OW Bunds over USTs and JGBs.
house_views.lazard: Geopolitics reshaping cross-border investment and M&A. Political capital increasingly required for deal execution. Europe-India FTA as major trade opportunity ($25.5bn annual uplift for UK alone). Focus on political risk premium in deal underwriting.
house_views.ned_davis_research: Only marketing/login pages available in dataset — no substantive calls extractable.
house_views.rosenberg_research: Only 2 articles in dataset, both thin. Rosenberg's well-known bear view (recession risk, overvalued equities) present but not freshly documented in this 30-day window.
house_views.capital_economics: Global investment growth slow: G7 capital spending +2% in 2026; US investment strength mostly AI-related; residential investment 11% below 2022 levels; China construction starts -80% from 2019 peak.
house_views.oxford_economics: AI mainstream but productivity boost still muted — breadth of adoption matters less than depth. Asia Chip Export Index +80.7% YoY value (Mar 2026). G7 capex +2% in 2026. Zero-migration scenario: Eurozone dependency ratio +9ppts by 2060.
house_views.iea: Greatest energy security threat in history. 14mb/d shut in; 1bn barrels cumulative loss; record emergency stock release; Brent ~1/3 above pre-conflict. Costa Azul LNG commissioning delayed to Q3. Global EV fleet to reach 510M by 2035.
house_views.wood_mackenzie: Gulf oil export recovery complex. 50% of global seaborne sulphur at risk; metals/mining supply chains structurally disrupted. UAE exit rattling OPEC's grip. Structural cost inflation for mine operators 'persistent.'
house_views.ecb: Financial Stability Review: geoeconomic risks heightened; bank P/B ratios receded from pre-GFC highs after Iran war; EGB yield curve steepened (NBFIs exposed); euro area inflation at 3.0% YoY (April).
house_views.bank_of_england: Tokenisation vision (Sarah Breeden); clinical bank supervision (Sam Woods); BPA market boom (354 transactions record in 2025, 45% of defined benefit schemes buy-out ready); 30yr gilts approaching 6%.
house_views.federal_reserve: Software inflation 9 std devs above historical mean (unprecedented); China Shock 2.0 ($1.2T surplus in 2025); labor market fragility documented. Taylor rule: policy 75-100bps too accommodative.
house_views.imf: Limited substantive content in dataset (teaser pages only). Consensus: global growth downgraded to ~2.5% in 2026 (UN figure per BNP) on Iran war; inflation upgraded +0.8ppts to 3.9%.
house_views.world_bank: Limited substantive content in dataset. Development finance channels disrupted by energy crisis. Middle East region hardest hit by growth downgrade.
house_views.brookings: US in worse strategic position after Iran war; Iran controls Hormuz; China benefiting from US distraction (Czin: 'America adrift risks falling behind China'); Taiwan partisan defense debate; Europe marginalized from Iran war diplomacy.
house_views.carnegie: Iran war = first AI war at unprecedented asymmetric scale; Continental Asia (Turkey to China) as new strategic arena; Europe risks becoming US/China 'colony'; Putin returned from China without Power of Siberia 2 deal; Russia increasingly dependent on China.
house_views.gavekal: US-China 'uneasy tactical stalemate' not breakthrough; China household NPLs climbing to 2.15%; migrant workers reverse-migrating; yuan appreciation cooling at CNY6.80; ceasefire extension discussions for Hormuz; next trade war flashpoint could be Europe.
house_views.coinshares: Bitcoin ETF outflows deepening: $2.54B over 2 weeks; largest BTC outflow of 2026. Risk-off broadened globally. YTD BTC flows compressed to $2.6B from $3.9B. CLARITY Act providing marginal support. ETH outflows $222-249M/week.
house_views.messari: CRCL (Circle) vs ARC: Circle Q1 reserve income $653M; ARC (Arc stablecoin) has monetization problem — at $0.001/transaction Visa-scale volume generates only $258M gross revenue vs Circle's existing run rate. State of XRP Q1: $775M ETF AUM, daily transactions +35% QoQ, RLUSD $340M market cap.
house_views.galaxy_digital: Crypto VC $4B Q1 (-50% QoQ, -16% deals QoQ); US = 70% of capital; Ethereum brain drain 8 departures in 5 months; Salomon Brothers lawsuit seeking Satoshi's $84B BTC; AI competing for marginal institutional dollars away from BTC ETFs.
house_views.delphi_digital: GPU access geopolitically mediated (HUMAIN $600B deal / Stargate UAE 200MW); Japan/Saudi tokenization race while China bans onshore RWA tokenization. L1 premium thesis potentially collapsing. Crypto becoming stock-picker's market.
house_views.chainalysis: Compliance baseline tightening (top-10% strictness in 2020 now near-median in 2026). UK sanctioned 18 crypto entities with Russia ties including HTX ($1.5B in Russia flows). A7 network moved $90B into Russia using crypto in 2025. Indirect exposure thresholds 10-20x more lenient than direct thresholds — exploitable gap.
house_views.t_rowe_price: International equities unlocking value; AI expanding across geographies and industries not just US; figure shows geographic AI adoption diversification as investment signal.
house_views.vanguard: Discipline prevails in geopolitical shocks — historical data shows selling into volatility destroys value; passive investors hold through. OW low-cost diversified exposures.
house_views.invesco: Limited specific calls; OW equities consistent with house view; monitoring Iran war trajectory closely.
house_views.man_group: Systematic strategies adapting to stagflationary regime; diversifying factor exposures; trend-following cautious on duration.
house_views.kkr: Private markets resilience; infrastructure as inflation hedge; credit selectivity essential in AI sector.
house_views.carlyle: PE exits under pressure (BASF Coatings acquisition cited as example of public-to-private opportunity); dry powder at record ($1.3T across industry); deployment pace picking up in H1 2026.
house_views.wisdomtree: Digital assets and tokenization as structural theme; gold maintaining reserve role despite pullback from $5,400 high.
house_views.bny: Settlement modernization; tokenization infrastructure as custody evolution; digital assets entering institutional era.
house_views.pgim: Fixed income opportunities in current environment; private credit selectivity; real assets for inflation protection.
house_views.franklin_templeton: OW EM equities; XRP ETF launched (XRPZ); fixed income opportunities in non-US developed markets.
house_views.amundi: European equities selective; OW EM bonds; transition finance (green → transition) as evolving framework.
house_views.rbc: Natural gas powers transition; Canada energy sector beneficiary of Iran shock; focus on North American energy infrastructure.
house_views.macquarie: Infrastructure assets as inflation hedge; energy transition accelerating despite short-term fossil fuel shock.
house_views.absolute_strategy: Independent bearish voice on equities; recession risk underpriced; AI productivity claims overblown in near term.
house_views.wells_fargo: US consumer resilience moderating; credit quality declining for lower-income segments; energy sector OW.
